data.content:
The build generates the file successfully but never adds it to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `@(Compile)` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but the generated file is never added to the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `@(Compile)` items in the target, after writing it. Something like:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```


This needs to go inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target right after the file is written out.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `@(Compile)` item group. MSBuild generated the file, but Roslyn had no idea it existed.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
